titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the David Pryce-Jones Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday May 14, 2019 at 03:00

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Boxes 49-51 (audiocassette): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 52 (videocassette): Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Joan Winterkorn on the James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection Fund, 2016.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: I. Family papers and correspondence, 1863-2000. II. Correspondence, 1960-2014. III. Notebooks and associated material, 1946-2000. IV. Writings, 1900-2015. V. Audio research materials, circa 1980s-1990s.

bioghist.head: David Pryce-Jones
bioghist.p: David-Pryce Jones (born 1936) is an English writer and editor. He is the son of Alan Pryce-Jones and Thérèse Fould-Springer Pryce-Jones. An author of fiction and non-fiction books, Pryce-Jones also served as a correspondent or literary editor for publications including the Financial Times, The Spectator, The Daily Telegraph, and the National Review.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: David Pryce-Jones Papers. James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The David Pryce-Jones papers, which span from 1863 to 2015, contain writings including drafts of novels and non-fiction works. The papers also contain correspondence, research materials, notebooks, ephemera, and audiovisual materials related to Pryce-Jones's life and work.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The David Pryce-Jones Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain organization found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, photographs, clippings, and other ephemera related to David Pryce-Jones, his family, and his life.

did.unittitle: War Time Correspondence from Cannes and Tangier - interfamily
Pryce-Jones, Alan to Nancy Caccia - letter

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains undated letter along with a toast composed by Alan Pryce-Jones (1937 December 28) and a guest list for David Pryce-Jones' wedding (circa 1959).

did.unittitle: Pryce-Jones, Alan and Thérèse ("Poppy") Fould-Spinger Pryce-Jones - letters, on the death of Nannie Stainer
To Mrs. S. M. Stainer - postcard, from Bad Ischl, Austria
Pryce-Jones, Thérèse Fould-Spinger, ("Poppy") to Jessie [Alice Wheeler] - letters
Wheeler, Jessie Alice to David Pryce-Jones - letters
Pryce-Jones, Thérèse Fould-Spinger, ("Poppy") to David Pryce-Jones at Castle Hill - letters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also contains war envelopes (ration booklets) and photographs.

did.unittitle: Pryce-Jones, Alan to his brother, Adrian Pryce-Jones - letters
Wooster, Mary ("Mitzi") Springer to Alan Pryce-Jones and Thérèse ("Poppy") Fould-Spinger Pryce-Jones - letters from Montréal
War time letters between Thérèse Fould-Spinger Pryce-Jones and her mother, Mary ("Mitzi") Springer Wooster
War time letters from Alan Pryce-Jones to Mary ("Mitzi") Springer Wooster
Pryce-Jones, Alan to Mary ("Mitzi") Springer Wooster - letters
To Mary ("Mitzi") Springer Wooster - war time letters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Nannie Stainer and Jessie Wheeler.

did.unittitle: [Pryce-Jones, Thérèse Fould-Spinger, ("Poppy")?] to [Mary ("Mitzi") Springer Wooster?] - letters
Correspondence regarding Alan Pryce-Jones' marriage to Mary Jean Thorne
Poems, by Thomas Gray - printed book
Pryce-Jones, Alan to David Pryce-Jones and Clarissa Caccia Pryce-Jones - letters
Wheeler, Jessie Alice and Marian Stainer to Thérèse ("Poppy") Fould-Spinger Pryce-Jones - letters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes a letter from Thérèse ("Poppy") Fould-Spinger Pryce-Jones to Nanny Stainer.

did.unittitle: Birthday notes and death bed letter to Mary ("Mitzi") Springer Wooster from Jessie Alice Wheeler
Character sketch of Thérèse ("Poppy") Fould-Spinger Pryce-Jones, by her sister Liliane de Rothschild
Pryce-Jones, David to Mary ("Mitzi") Springer Wooster - letters from Israel
Forster, Grace to Thérèse ("Poppy") Fould-Spinger Pryce-Jones - letter

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contain reports on David Pryce-Jones in Tangier.

did.unittitle: Propper de Callejon, Eduardo to Thérèse ("Poppy") Fould-Spinger Pryce-Jones - letters from Tangier

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Mary ("Mitzi") Springer Wooster, Elie de Rothschild, Adrian Pryce-Jones and the Welsh Guards, Jessie Alice Wheeler, Vere Dawnay Pryce-Jones (David Pryce-Jones' grandmother) and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Alan Pryce-Jones, Thérèse ("Poppy") Fould-Spinger Pryce-Jones, and the Fould-Springer family. Also includes photographs, clippings and printed material.

did.unittitle: Letters and postcards from Mary ("Mitzi") Wooster

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contents collected by Jessica Pryce-Jones.

did.unittitle: Letters to Marion Vere Dawnay Pryce-Jones

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include the children of Mary ("Mitzi") Springer Wooster.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a photograph of Eugène Fould (1898), a cloth embroidery, newspaper clippings, correspondence and postcards from Eugène Fould, and other papers.

did.unittitle: Letters from Thérèse Fould-Spinger Pryce-Jones,("Poppy"), to her mother Mary ("Mitzi") Springer Wooster
Autobiographical notes regarding the diaries of Mary ("Mitzi") Springer Wooster

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: "Lettre de Papa"; a photocopy of a May 1944 statement from Philippe Pétain; a revue program.

did.unittitle: "Tante Lizzie" (Vicomtesse de Nantois, née Fould) to Thérèse ("Poppy") Fould-Spinger Pryce-Jones - letters
Memoirs of "Tante Lizzie" (Vicomtesse de Nantois, née Fould)
Watercolor painting of Venice, by JOB

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also contains an etching of Puvis de Chavannes's The Sacred Wood Cherished by the Arts and the Muses, created by Braun, Clément et Cie.

did.unittitle: "À Mitzi avec toute ma tendress - Elisabeth" - corrected typescript
Souvenirs de Guerre, notes d'occupation - corrected typescripts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a family history of the Fould family.

did.unittitle: Pryce-Jones, Alan to Marion Vere Dawnay Pryce-Jones - letters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains, letters, a postcard, and portrait photographs of family members.

did.unittitle: Wooster, Frank to Alan Pryce-Jones, from Canada - letter

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains letters, postcards and newspaper clippings. Correspondents include Alan Pryce-Jones, Henry Morris Pryce-Jones, and Marion Vere Dawnay Pryce-Jones.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes newspaper clippings and postcards.

did.unittitle: Pryce-Jones, Alan to Thérèse ("Poppy") Fould-Springer Pryce-Jones - letters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains letters, postcards and clippings. Also contains a handwritten poem entitled "September 13 1943."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, primarily from Alan Pryce-Jones.

did.unittitle: Pryce-Jones, Alan to Thérèse ("Poppy") Fould-Springer Pryce-Jones - letters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also contains clippings.

did.unittitle: Pryce-Jones, Alan to Thérèse ("Poppy") Fould-Springer Pryce-Jones - letters
Pryce-Jones, Alan - Rome and Vienna letters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Thérèse ("Poppy") Fould-Springer Pryce-Jones.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Thérèse ("Poppy") Fould-Springer Pryce-Jones.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Thérèse ("Poppy") Fould-Springer Pryce-Jones.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains childhood photographs, notebooks and journals, writings and notes, correspondence, papers relating to Alan Pryce-Jones including his birth certificate, and school papers including certificates, report cards, and associated writings.

did.unittitle: Wooster, Mary ("Mitzi") Springer - letters, from Meidling
Report on Mary ("Mitzi") Springer Wooster's properties in war

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains letters, postcards, clippings, a ration book, and photographs.

did.unittitle: Pryce-Jones, Thérèse Fould-Springer to Mary ("Mitzi") Springer Wooster - letters
Pryce-Jones, David and Clarissa Caccia Pryce-Jones - correspondence
Pryce-Jones, David to Mary ("Mitzi") Springer Wooster - letters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also contains postcards.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: clippings and notes, possibly relating to geneological research.

did.unittitle: "Comédie pour les 80 ans de Bon Papa"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a small play, letters, and a copy of a war memoir by Louise Emile Fould Halphen (1862-1945), from Châteauroux (1943).

did.unittitle: Rapport du Hofrichter Ladislaus Hajdu sur la propriété située sur la Theiss du 8 octorbre 1944-30 octobre 1945

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a report by Hofrichter Ladislaus Hajdu on the property located on the Theiss River.

did.unittitle: Halphen, Louise Emile Foulds - war memoir

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a copy of a war memoir by Louise Emile Fould Halphen (1862-1945), from Châteauroux (1943).

did.unittitle: Fould-Springer, Max - letters from Magdalen College, Oxford
Springer, Gustave, Eugène Fould and Mary ("Mitzi") Springer Wooster - letters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence regarding the wedding of Mary ("Mitzi") Springer Wooster and Eugène Fould, as well as material regarding the deaths of Charles Ephrussi and Robert Fould.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains letters and postcards,along with Mary ("Mitzi") Springer Wooster's diary entries.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a typed family history of the Fould family, from 1738 to 1944.

did.unittitle: As Fine As Melanctha - typed forward by Gertrude Stein, with letter

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a letter from Natalie Clifford Barney to Max Fould.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials regarding Eugène Fould's military service and schooling.

did.unittitle: Wooster, Mary ("Mitzi") Springer to Eugène Fould - letters
Photographs, letters and documents of the Fould-Springer family

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted Fragile Material. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains three glass slides of an unsigned letter.

did.unittitle: "Lettres d'Eugène et de moi a ja mere..."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains writings of Elisabeth de Nantois-Fould and Eugène Fould-Springer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Léon Fould, Elisabeth Fould de Nantois, and Joseph de la Goublaye de Nantois.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains documents regarding the business run by Henri-Jules Fould and Léon Fould.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence with Rudolf Springer, Mary ("Mitzi") Springer Wooster, and Gustav Springer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain organization found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence to and from David Pryce-Jones, along with postcards, clippings, and related ephemera.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains incoming letters for David Pryce-Jones.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, postcards, magazines, photocopies, clippings, a photograph jigsaw puzzle, writings, and email printouts.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain the order found at time of acquisition.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain the order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains typescripts, drafts, proofs and other materials related to David Pryce-Jones's writings.

George Weidenfeld: A Seventieth Birthday Tribute
The Generation of Revenge; The White Devil

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains drafts, clippings, and correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains research notes and sources.

did.unittitle: Treason of the Heart - research notes
Paris in the Third Reich - research materials and notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains John Mortimer's dramatisation of David Pryce-Jones' Unity Mitford.

did.unittitle: The War that Never Was - reviews
Paris in the Third Reich - reviews

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a corrected galley proof, with a title page setting copy.

did.unittitle: The War that Never Was - corrected proof
Paris in the Third Reich - research material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photostats of newspapers, letters and other research material.

did.unittitle: From Iowa to Kiowa - corrected typescript and notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains clippings regarding Sands of Summer, Graham Greene, and Age of Austerity.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains an article on aspiring writers conferring with David Pryce-Jones at Mont Chateau Lodge in 1969.

did.unittitle: Voltaire: The Princess of Babylon, translated by David Pryce-Jones - corrected typescript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains typescripts, handwritten notes, proofs, and clippings.

did.unittitle: The Sands of Summer - corrected typescript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains material related to the American edition of The Stranger's View (1967) and The Hungarian Revolution (1969).

did.unittitle: Mayday in Pistiany and Next Generation - corrected typescripts
Newspaper clippings regarding The Stranger's View
Owls & Satyrs - review by Roald Sörensen

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains newspaper clippings regarding Running Away (1971), The Face of Defeat (1972), and Evelyn Waugh & His world (1973).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains typescripts of "Keeping Up with the Times," "Some Lectures in the Provinces," "Cara Mia," "The Family Plate," "As You Like It," "Having A Lovely Time. Wish You Were Here," "Soldier of Fortune," "Last Thing At Night," and other stories. Also contains newspaper clippings related to "Like Father Like Son" and "The Bad Hat."

did.unittitle: The Stranger's View - drafts, typescripts, and corrected proofs
Graham Greene - drafts and corrected typescript
La Vie Étrange des Objets - translation drafts and corrected typescript
Haifa, Tel Aviv, Jerusalem: A combined profile - corrected typescript
Songs for an Office: A Modern Masque - - corrected poem typescripts
The Stranger's View - corrected printer's proof
Paris in the Third Reich - reviews
The Stranger's View - book proofs and corrected typescripts
Cyril Connolly: Journal & Memoir - uncorrected proof
The Face of Defeat - uncorrected proof
The Strange Death of the Soviet Empire - uncorrected proof
Betrayal: France, the Arabs, and the Jews - uncorrected proof
The War that Never Was - drafts and corrected typescripts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains handwritten notes, photostats, and research materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains newspaper clippings and photocopies of reviews regarding Unity Mitford (1976), as well as letters of support from Alan Pryce-Jones and Jessica Mitford.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains newspaper and magazine articles regarding Unity Mitford, as well as David Pryce-Jones' book about her (1976).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Unity Mitford research material, specifically involving the Der Stürmer letter from July 1935.

did.unittitle: Unity Mitford - article and David Pryce-Jones diary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a handwritten diary by David Pryce-Jones, kept while the Unity Mitford book was about to be published. Also contains a typescript article regarding Unity Mitford.

did.unittitle: Unity Mitford: A Quest - book proof

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photoprints of 1930s photographs of Unity Mitford and her family, used in the creation of David Pryce-Jones' 1976 book.

did.unittitle: The Strange Death of the Soviet Union - notes and clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains handwritten notes, photocopies, and typescripts.

accessrestrict.p: Restricted Fragile Material. Reference copies of audiovisual material may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains old photographs and photoprints from the American Colony, Jerusalem.

did.unittitle: Photographs and photoprints of Evelyn Waugh

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted Fragile Material. Reference copies of audiovisual material may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of aquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains audiocassettes featuring material generally pertaining to Eastern Europe and the Soviet Union.

did.unittitle: Gelu Voican II: trail of Ceaușescu
Tape I - Kalman Kulshar [Kálmán Kulcsár] - Minister of Justice
Side A - 2nd half [Szczes?] / Side B - Nyous tape I
Side I - Gal / Side II - [Malzin Szcześ?] I
